# Kevin Astuhuaman Full Machine Profile

Last updated: June 20, 2026, Pacific time.

## Identity

Kevin Astuhuaman is an AI Product Manager, Technical PM, and Berkeley Haas MBA based in the Bay Area. His public recruiting thesis is: hands-on AI product operator who can personally build systems and operate inside enterprise-scale product environments.

## Current Positioning

AI Product Manager building production AI-agent systems across recruiting automation, observability, and fintech.

## Education

- UC Berkeley Haas, MBA, May 2026, STEM-designated.
- UC Berkeley Haas, AI for Business Graduate Certificate, first cohort.
- PUCP, BS Industrial Engineering.

## Experience Anchors

### Trackly

Trackly is a real-time job-search agent Kevin built during his own job hunt. It monitors direct company career pages and exposes job-search signal through web, iOS, macOS, CLI, and MCP surfaces.

Public evidence:
- Live site: https://usetrackly.app
- App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trackly-apply-first/id6758267565
- CLI + MCP repo: https://github.com/trackly-app/trackly-cli

Claims:
- 1,900+ companies monitored and 128,975+ roles shown on the public Trackly site.
- 500+ monthly active users from the latest internal snapshot.
- Featured by Berkeley Haas and expanding into MBA job-search workflows. Treat exact institutional claims as proof-led and cite them when public URLs are available.

### PayPal

Kevin was a Technical Product Manager MBA Intern on Checkout in 2025. Resume-approved summary: built AI observability tooling across 15+ internal checkout systems, reducing issue detection time by 75% in the internship project workflow.

Public-safe wording:
- "Built AI observability tooling across 15+ internal checkout systems."
- "Reduced issue detection time by 75% in the internship project workflow."
- "$100M target uplift" must be phrased as modeled or targeted opportunity, not achieved impact.

Do not publish internal screenshots, logs, system diagrams, or confidential product names.

### BCP / Credicorp

Kevin worked on digital-first financial products including lending, insurance, and payments for small businesses and underserved customers.

Public-safe wording:
- "Worked on digital-first financial products tied to a $620M ARR/P&L portfolio."
- "Expanded access for 100K+ small businesses and underserved customers."

Public evidence:
- PUCP profile: https://facultad-ciencias-ingenieria.pucp.edu.pe/2024/10/07/kevin-astuhuaman-egresado-de-ingenieria-industrial-admitido-a-maestria-en-berkeley/

## Recruiter Summary

Kevin is a Berkeley Haas MBA and AI Product Manager who combines enterprise AI observability, fintech product scale, and hands-on product building. His strongest proof point is Trackly, a live job-search agent with web, iOS, macOS, CLI, and MCP surfaces. His enterprise proof point is PayPal Checkout AI observability across 15+ systems. His operating-scale proof point is BCP/Credicorp digital-first financial products for 100K+ SMBs and underserved customers.

## Constraints

- No em dashes in public copy.
- Avoid legally imprecise partnership wording.
- No inflated superlative phrasing.
- Preserve work-authorization wording as eligibility, not a legal guarantee.
- Keep PayPal and employer details public-safe.
